items.title: Boeing expected to fly 777-9 for first time in nearly five years, Air Current editor says
items.description: Boeing is expected to conduct the first flight of its 777-9 jet in about five years as early as August 5, Air Current editor Jon Ostrower said on Monday in a post on X, citing two people familiar with the matter. Reuters could not independently verify the report.

items.title: Air Lease profit, revenue rise on insurance payout and aircraft demand
items.description: (Reuters) -Aircraft lessor Air Lease Corp reported a rise in second-quarter profit and revenue on Monday, due to a $344 million insurance claim settlement plus robust aircraft rental demand.  Boeing and Airbus plan to increase output, but "even with those increases, which have not all been achieved by any certainty, there is still a shortfall in supply for the next three to five years," Air Lease CEO John Plueger said during a conference call with investment analysts on Monday.  The company confirmed that it canceled its 2021 order for seven Airbus A350 freighters last month.

items.title: Joby Stock Jumps; These Defense Stocks Rise
items.description: Joby stock gains on deal for Blade's passenger air taxi business. Boeing faces new strike. Axon clears earnings views.

items.title: These Stocks Moved the Most Today: Palantir, Tesla, Berkshire Hathaway, ON Semiconductor, Joby, D-Wave, and More
items.description: Berkshire Hathaway stock fell after second-quarter operating profit after tax fell 4% in the period, while Palantir shares were rising ahead of earnings.
